name: Andouille in Puff Pastry
recipeInstructions:
To cook the sausage, slit the skin at several points with a knife to prevent it from splitting while cooking. Place in a pot of cold water and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes. Remove, allow to cool on a cloth, then carefully remove skin with a knife. Chop coarsely.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Spread puff pastry sheets as directed. Cut into 4-inch squares.
Place 1 tbsp of Andouille in the center of each square. For variety, place 1/3 of the appetizers with 1/2 tbsp mustard, 1/3 with 1 cup grated cheese, and leave 1/3 plain. Brush edges with beaten egg white, fold into triangles and press edges together with a fork to seal and flute. Brush tops with beaten egg white. Bake at 425°F for about 15 minutes or until golden brown.
VARIATION-Fresh pork or Italian sausage may be substituted for the Andouille. Source: Copeland's. NOLA
